data.title: Eni, YPF sign deal to develop Argentina LNG project
data.description:
Italy's Eni (NYSE:E) and Argentina's YPF (NYSE:YPF) said Friday they signed an agreement to develop the Argentina liquefied natural gas export project, underpinned by natural gas from the Vaca Muerta shale formation.

The Argentina LNG project is designed to export up to 30M tons/year of LNG by 2030, using a phased approach.

The agreement, which builds on a memorandum of understanding signed in April, is a step toward a final investment decision that could be finalized by year-end, YPF (NYSE:YPF) CEO Horacio Marín said.

Eni (NYSE:E) said it was selected as a strategic partner for Argentina LNG "due to its specific and distinctive know-how developed during its FLNG projects in Congo and Mozambique and its global leadership in the construction of projects with similar technological requirements."

The project would transport natural gas from the Neuquen basin to a coastal site in Rio Negro, where it will be liquefied using floating liquefaction vessels for overseas export; Eni (E) said it would initially provide two FLNG units with a combined capacity of 12M tons/year.

data.title: U.S. Supreme Court grants DOGE access to social security data
data.description:
The U.S. Supreme Court on Friday allowed the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) access to the social security information of millions of Americans by lifting a restriction placed earlier by a lower district court. 

DOGE was set up under the leadership of Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) chief executive Elon Musk to cut federal spending. Musk exited the agency last week, and has since then spectacularly fallen out with U.S. President Donald Trump in a public verbal spat.

When DOGE was established in January by Trump, his executive order required other government agencies to allow employees "full and prompt access to all unclassified agency records, software systems, and IT systems." But U.S. District Judge Ellen Hollander in April ordered the Social Security Administration (SSA) from granting DOGE affiliates access to various records. 

The Trump administration appealed to the 4th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals to stay Hollander's order, but lost that request by a vote of 9-6. It then turned to the Supreme Court.  

"When considering whether to grant a stay, this Court looks to four factors: '(1) whether the stay applicant has made a strong showing that he is likely to succeed on the merits; (2) whether the applicant will be irreparably injured absent a stay; (3) whether issuance of the stay will substantially injure the other parties interested in the proceeding; and (4) where the public interest lies,'" the Supreme Court's unsigned order said. 

"After review, we determine that the application of these factors in this case warrants granting the requested stay. We conclude that, under the present circumstances, SSA may proceed to afford members of the SSA DOGE Team access to the agency records in question in order for those members to do their work," the order added.

There were three dissenting opinions among the Supreme Court judges, including Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson.

"The Government wants to give DOGE unfettered access to this personal, non-anonymized information right now—before the courts have time to assess whether DOGE’s access is lawful. So it asks this Court to stay a lower court’s decision to place temporary and qualified limits on DOGE’s data access while litigation challenging DOGE’s authority to access the data is pending," Jackson wrote.

"But the Government fails to substantiate its stay request by showing that it or the public will suffer irreparable harm absent this Court’s intervention. In essence, the 'urgency' underlying the Government’s stay application is the mere fact that it cannot be bothered to wait for the litigation process to play out before proceeding as it wishes," Jackson added. 

Wall Street has largely ignored the noise around DOGE and Elon Musk, though his verbal spat with Trump did weigh on investor sentiment for Tesla's (TSLA) shares, with the stock eventually shedding more than $150B in market cap.

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500):  (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

More on DOGE and Elon Musk

  • Easterly Government sees tailwinds from DOGE efforts
  • Trump, Musk in public spat over spending bill; Tesla stock gets hit in the aftermath
  • Trump asks Congress to formally approve DOGE-identified cuts
  • Elon Musk bashes Trump's 'big, beautiful bill'
  • Trump administration targets contracts with tech firms for more federal savings - WSJ

data.title: Tenaris to commence $600 million first tranche of $1.2 billion share buyback program
data.description:
• Tenaris S.A. (NYSE:TS)  said on Friday that it has entered into a non-discretionary buyback agreement with a primary financial institution pursuant to its previously announced share buyback program covering up to $1.2 billion.
  • The Bank will make its trading decisions concerning the timing of the purchases of Tenaris’s ordinary shares independently of and uninfluenced by Tenaris, highlighted the company. 
  • "The first tranche of the program will cover up to $600 million (excluding customary transaction fees) and will start on June 9, 2025, and end no later than December 8, 2025", said the company, adding that, "Ordinary shares purchased under the Program will be cancelled in due course."
  • TS +0.98% after hours to $35.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Gold slips as stronger than expected U.S. jobs data seen dimming chances for rate cuts
data.description:
Gold futures fell on Friday after the U.S. Labor Department reported slightly stronger than expected jobs data that dampened hopes the Federal Reserve would lower rates in the near term, while silver added to its rally that has seen the metal keep hitting fresh 13-year highs.

Non-farm payrolls rose by 139K in May, slowing a bit from the 147K jobs added in April, indicating employers remain cautious given the uncertain economic outlook, which analysts said was a negative for the gold market because the data suggests that the Fed is more likely to stay on hold for a while longer; the data also strengthened the U.S. dollar, an alternative safe-haven asset.

Gold is considered a hedge against inflation and geopolitical uncertainty, but it yields no interest so higher rates reduce its investment appeal.

Gold gained on the week from renewed trade tensions, geopolitical risks and a weaker U.S. dollar, as President Trump doubled existing steel and aluminum tariffs to 50%, the Russia-Ukraine war intensified, and the dollar declined, and analysts expect volatility will persist as global markets remain on edge.

Gold-backed ETFs globally reported modest outflows in May for the first time in five months, according to the World Gold Council, as investors took profits, but flows of metal into gold-backed funds remain positive for the year at 322 metric tons.

Meanwhile, silver futures are trading at their best level in more than 13 years, as other precious metal types are being increasingly chosen for safe-haven heding with gold recently setting new all-time highs.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for June delivery ended +1% to $3,322.70 this week, and front-month Comex June silver (XAGUSD:CUR) closed +9.5% for the week at $36.025/oz, its highest settlement value since February 28, 2012; on Friday, gold fell 0.8% and silver gained 0.9%.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Kovo+ announces CFO resignation
data.description:
• Kovo+ (TSXV:KOVO:CA) announced that Mark Detz has resigned as chief financial officer of the company, and Kovo accepted his resignation effective as of June 4, 2024.
  • Following a confidential recruitment process, the company has identified and selected a candidate to succeed Detz as chief financial offer and expects to announce the appointment in due course.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: High roller receives non-compliance notice from NYSE
data.description:
• High Roller Technologies (NYSE:ROLR) said on Friday that the company was notified by NYSE American that the company is not in compliance with NYSE's minimum stockholders' equity rule due to reporting of stockholders’ equity of approximately $2.8 million, and it has reported losses from continuing operations and/or net losses in three of its four most recent fiscal years ended December 31, 2024.
  • The notice stated that the company must submit a compliance plan by July 4, 2025, that demonstrates how it intends to regain compliance with the continued listing standards within 18 months of the receipt of the notice, or December 4, 2026. 
  • The company said it intends to develop and submit to the NYSE American such a plan. 
  • During this period, the company said, its common stock will continue to be listed on the NYSE American and trade as usual subject to compliance with other NYSE American listing requirements.
  • ROLR -1.39% after hours to $2.83.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Oceaneering CFO Alan Curtis to retire
data.description:
• Oceaneering International (NYSE:OII) on Friday said Alan Curtis, Senior Vice President & CFO, has announced his decision to retire from his position effective January 1, 2026.
  • Curtis began his career with Oceaneering in 1995 as a Financial Controller and has served in a variety of roles, including most recently as CFO since 2015.
  • Michael Sumruld will join Oceaneering as Senior Vice President of Finance on September 1, 2025, and is expected to later succeed Curtis as CFO upon his retirement.
  • Sumruld most recently served as Senior Vice President and CFO for Parker Drilling Company from October 2017 until its sale to Nabors Industries in March 2025. Prior to his time at Parker Drilling, Sumruld held positions at LyondellBasell Industries and at Baker Hughes.
  • Source: Press Release
